  • 湖州长兴州金沙泉 - Location and name of the spring.
  • 唐 - The Tang dynasty.
  • 宋季 - The late Song dynasty.
  • 至元十五年 - The 15th year of the Zhiyuan era of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 中书省 - The Central Secretariat of the Yuan dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 血殷地 - A vivid expression meaning 'the blood stained the ground,' used to describe a scene of violence or death.
  • 无噍类 - Means 'no survivors' or 'no living thing,' indicating total destruction.
  • 周章振掉 - Describes a state of panic or agitation, with '周章' meaning 'to be in a state of confusion' and '振掉' meaning 'to shake or tremble.'
  • 谋所以免祸者 - Means 'planning how to avoid disaster,' indicating efforts to escape harm.
  • 毁巢破鷇 - Refers to destroying nests and breaking eggs, symbolizing the destruction of future generations.
  • 空所积以奉爽鸠 - Means 'emptying what they have accumulated to serve the hawk,' indicating the act of giving up resources to appease a powerful entity.
  • 以赂雕使勿执 - Means 'to bribe the hawk to prevent it from seizing,' indicating an attempt to buy protection.
  • 雕之势益张 - Means 'the hawk's power becomes increasingly strong,' indicating the growing dominance of a powerful entity.
  • 众禽之生理日蹙 - Means 'the survival of other birds becomes increasingly difficult,' indicating the worsening situation for the weaker entities.
  • 爪距稍利者慕雕所为 - Means 'those with slightly sharper claws envy the hawk's actions,' indicating the admiration of the powerful by the weak.
  • 起而效之 - Means 'rise up and emulate it,' indicating the imitation of the powerful.
  • 馁死于草莽 - Means 'starve to death in the wilderness,' indicating a tragic end.
  • 相借也 - Means 'rely on each other,' indicating mutual dependence.
  • 凤凰始忧之 - Means 'the phoenix begins to worry about it,' indicating the concern of a wise or noble entity.
  • 蓬莱之颠有胎仙焉 - Means 'there is a fetus of a fairy at the top of Penglai,' indicating a mythical place and a divine being.
  • 胎仙名号青田翁 - Means 'the fetus of a fairy is named  and called Qingtian Old Man,' indicating the name and title of the divine being.
  • 廉介而洁白 - Means 'honest and pure,' describing the moral qualities of the divine being.
  • 和平而好生 - Means 'peaceful and fond of life,' describing the character of the divine being.
  • 徴爽鸠使乘轩而治之 - Means 'summon the hawk and send  to govern with a carriage,' indicating the appointment of a wise figure to manage affairs.
  • 夫雕其始一而已 - Means 'the hawk was originally just one,' indicating the initial state of the powerful entity.
  • 自子之不戒而使之蔓延 - Means 'because you did not guard against it and allowed it to spread,' indicating the cause of the problem.
  • 今之为雕者何其多耶 - Means 'why are there so many hawks now?' indicating the question about the proliferation of the powerful entity.
  • 昔之雕名雕字雕形雕性雕本为雕者也 - Means 'the hawk was originally named, written, shaped, and characterized as a hawk,' indicating the original identity of the powerful entity.
  • 今有非雕而雕者何也 - Means 'why are there those who are not hawks but act like hawks?' indicating the question about the imitation of the powerful entity.
  • 雕则得食不雕则不得食 - Means 'those who act like hawks get food, those who do not act like hawks do not get food,' indicating the consequences of imitation.
  • 雕则有利而无害不雕则利未见而害 - Means 'those who act like hawks have benefits without harm, those who do not act like hawks do not see benefits but suffer harm,' indicating the outcomes of different choices.

  • 凤凰曰善奏请于帝 - Compound expression where '凤凰曰善' means 'the phoenix said, 'Good,' and '奏请于帝' means 'to petition the emperor.'

  • 雕之徒尽毙勑天下无留雕 - Compound expression where '雕之徒尽毙' means 'all the followers of the eagles were killed,' and '勑天下无留雕' means 'commanding the world to eliminate all eagles.'
  • 其余党皆屏迹匿形不敢出众禽始得安于生养以尽其天年 - Compound expression where '其余党皆屏迹匿形' means 'the remaining followers all hid their traces and forms,' '不敢出众禽' means 'did not dare to show themselves among the other birds,' and '始得安于生养以尽其天年' means 'thus they could live in peace and fulfill their natural lifespan.'
  • 此皆少皥氏之恩凤凰与之力也 - Compound expression where '此皆少皥氏之恩' means 'all this is due to the grace of the Shao Hao,' '凤凰与之力也' means 'the power of the phoenix and the phoenix's helper.'
  • 太史公曰雕奸禽也暴恶受诛固宜 - Compound expression where '太史公曰' means 'the historian says,' '雕奸禽也' means 'eagles are wicked birds,' '暴恶受诛' means 'cruel and evil, deserving punishment,' and '固宜' means 'it is indeed fitting.'
  • 吾独惧今之人子务养雕意有所欲举雕而放之 - Compound expression where '吾独惧' means 'I alone fear,' '今之人子' means 'modern people,' '务养雕' means 'devote themselves to raising eagles,' '意有所欲' means 'if they have a desire,' and '举雕而放之' means 'to raise and release the eagles.'
  • 求众禽之血肉以肥其躯殊不知少皥氏之戒也 - Compound expression where '求众禽之血肉' means 'seeking the blood and flesh of other birds,' '以肥其躯' means 'to fatten their bodies,' and '殊不知少皥氏之戒也' means 'but do not know the warning of Shao Hao.'
  • 嗟夫害物而日益者刑虽未及天必谴之其雕岂足恤哉 - Compound expression where '嗟夫' means 'Alas,' '害物而日益者' means 'those who harm others and grow increasingly wicked,' '刑虽未及' means 'although punishment has not yet been meted out,' '天必谴之' means 'the heavens will surely punish them,' and '其雕岂足恤哉' means 'how can such eagles be pitied?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 郑卫之淫声 - Refers to the music of the states of Zheng and Wei, which were known for their licentious and sensuous melodies. This alludes to the idea that such music is inappropriate for sacred or noble purposes.
  • 雅乐 - Refers to the ancient Chinese ceremonial music used in rituals and court ceremonies, emphasizing its solemn and dignified nature.
  • 近之也 - A phrase indicating that the human voice is closer to the ideal of musical expression, emphasizing its natural and direct appeal.
  • 成文章曰乐府 - Refers to the genre of poetry and music known as *Yuefu*, which includes both songs and poems set to music.
  • 有尾声曰套数 - Refers to the structure of *Yuefu* poetry, which often includes a concluding section called the *wailüe* (tail sound).
  • 时行小令曰叶儿 - Refers to the popular short lyrics or ballads of the time, known as *yè* (leaf), which were often set to music.
  • 套数当有乐府气味 - Suggests that *Yuefu* poetry should have a distinct musical quality or flavor.
  • 乐府不可似套数 - Emphasizes that *Yuefu* poetry should not be confused with the more structured and formal *taoshu* (set numbers).
  • 词山曲海 - A metaphorical expression describing the vast and diverse body of poetry and music, similar to 'a mountain of words and a sea of songs'.
  • 千生万熟 - A phrase indicating that a large number of works have been written and refined over time, emphasizing the depth and breadth of literary tradition.
  • 三千小令 - Refers to the large number of short lyrics or ballads that have been composed.
  • 四十大曲 - Refers to the large number of long and complex musical compositions that have been created.
  • 庄蓼塘藏书 - Refers to the library of Zhuang Liao Tang, a notable collector of books in the Song Dynasty.
  • 松江府上海县青龙镇 - Refers to the location of Zhuang Liao Tang's residence, which was in Qinglong Town, Shanghai County, Songjiang Prefecture.
  • 宋秘书小史 - Refers to a minor official in the Song Dynasty's imperial secretariat.
  • 山经地志 - Refers to geographical and topographical texts, including the *Shan Jing* (Classic of Mountains) and *Di Zhi* (Geographical Records).
  • 医卜方伎 - Refers to medical, divination, and technical texts, including works on medicine, astrology, and practical skills.
  • 稗官小说 - Refers to minor or unofficial historical and fictional works, including *Baiguan* (minor officials' records) and *Xiaoshuo* (short stories).
  • 书目以甲乙分十门 - Refers to the classification of books into ten categories, using the system of *Jia* (甲) and *Yi* (乙) to denote different sections.
  • 子孙不知保惜 - Indicates that Zhuang Liao Tang's descendants did not take proper care of his library.
  • 或供饮博之需 - Suggests that some books were used for gambling or entertainment.
  • 或应糊覆之用 - Indicates that some books were used for practical purposes, such as making paper or glue.
  • 编帙散乱 - Describes the state of the books, which were disorganized and in disarray.
  • 所存无几 - Indicates that very few books remained after the damage and loss.
  • 至正六年 - Refers to the year 1346 in the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 朝廷开局修宋辽金三史 - Refers to the imperial order to compile the histories of the Song, Liao, and Jin dynasties.
  • 诏求遗书 - Indicates that the government issued a decree to collect any remaining books and manuscripts.
  • 有以书献者予一官 - Refers to the reward of an official position for anyone who contributed books to the project.
  • 江南藏书多者止三家 - Indicates that there were only three major book collectors in southern China at the time.
  • 庄其一也 - Refers to Zhuang Liao Tang as one of these major collectors.
  • 继命危学士朴特来取其家 - Refers to the appointment of Wei Xueshi Pu to collect the books from Zhuang Liao Tang's family.
  • 虑恐兵遁图防干犯禁条 - Indicates that the books were destroyed to avoid potential legal or military complications.
  • 悉付祝融氏 - Refers to the act of burning the books, with *Zhuruo Shi* (the god of fire) symbolizing the act of destruction.
  • 及收拾烬余存者又无几矣 - Indicates that even the remaining ashes and remnants were few.
  • 其孙羣玉悉载入京觊领恩泽 - Refers to Zhuang Liao Tang's grandson, Qunyu, who transported the remaining books to the capital in hopes of receiving a reward.
  • 宿畱日乆仍布衣而归 - Indicates that despite his efforts, Qunyu returned home in his ordinary clothes, having received no reward.
  • 书之不幸如此 - Concludes that the fate of the books was unfortunate.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 买假山 - Literally 'buy a rockwork,' referring to purchasing a rock garden or artificial mountain.
  • 移置家园 - Means 'move and place in one's home garden,' indicating the relocation of the rockwork to a private garden.
  • 邀渊白观之 - Means 'invite Yuan Bai to view it,' where '渊白' is a name, likely a person of some importance or acquaintance of the speaker.
  • 此公族中之物 - Means 'this is an object from the public domain' or 'this belongs to the community,' suggesting that the rockwork was originally part of a public or communal space.
  • 东搬西倒陈 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous but can be interpreted as 'Tian moved it east and west,' implying that the person named Tian (陈) moved the rockwork around, possibly in a haphazard manner.
  • 绝嗣 - Means 'to become extinct' or 'to have no descendants,' indicating the end of a family line.
  • 巨富 - Means 'extremely wealthy,' describing the financial status of the family.
  • 妬悍无比 - Means 'extremely jealous and fierce,' describing the wife's character.
  • 仅有一女 - Means 'only has one daughter,' indicating the family's child status.
  • 赘谢季初为壻 - Means 'Xie Jichu was taken as a son-in-law,' indicating the arrangement of a marriage.
  • 纳一妾于嘉兴外舍得男 - Means 'took a concubine who gave birth to a son in Jiaxing,' indicating the acquisition of a concubine and her child.
  • 蚤夜怒詈 - Means 'angry and cursing day and night,' describing the wife's constant anger and verbal abuse.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The term '解语杯' is a playful and metaphorical name, which requires careful translation to capture its dual meaning of a 'speaking cup' and a cup that interacts with the drinkers.
  • The sentence '酒半折正开荷花置小金巵于其中命歌姬捧以行酒' is a complex sequence of actions, requiring clear and logical translation to maintain the flow and meaning.
  • The detailed description of the drinking ritual '客就姬取花左手执枝右手分开花瓣以口就饮' must be translated to preserve the elegance and grace of the gesture.
  • The comparison to the refined practice of drinking from a bamboo tube '其风致又过碧筩逺甚' must be clearly conveyed to highlight the superior elegance of the lotus cup.
  • The playful nature of the inscription '张句曲戯题黄大痴小像' must be captured in the translation to convey the lighthearted and artistic tone.
  • The humorous and exaggerated description of Huang Dacheng's character '全真家数禅和口鼓贫子骨头吏员臓腑' requires careful handling to preserve the intended effect.
  • The vivid and detailed description of Zhu Zhongyi '七尺躯威仪济济三寸舌是非风起一双眼看人做官两脚沿门报喜' must be translated to maintain the impact and imagery.
  • The satirical nature of the composition '张明善作北乐府水仙子讥时' must be clearly conveyed to capture the critical and humorous intent.
  • The satirical description of the political elite '铺眉苦眼早三公裸袖揎拳享万钟胡言乱语成时用大纲来都是烘' must be translated to preserve the biting and critical tone.
  • The metaphors for various types of people '五眼鸡岐山鸣凤两头蛇南阳卧龙三脚猫渭水非熊' must be carefully translated to maintain their symbolic and cultural significance.
